_comment: Data-driven credential-file corpus for env-guard.py (loaded from beside the hook, the popular-packages.json precedent; CE_GUARDED_SECRETS overrides the path for tests/operators). Two entry kinds: `home_path` denies a home-anchored credential store regardless of cwd (a credential store is never workspace content); `basename` applies the outside-the-workspace containment test so a project's own file (e.g. its .env / .npmrc) stays readable. `pattern` is an fnmatch glob (home_path matches the realpath'd absolute path; basename matches the filename only). `decision` is OPTIONAL and defaults to env-guard.py's ENV_FILE constant (currently "deny") — the shipped entries omit it so that constant stays the single flip-to-ask lever the deny message documents. A missing or malformed corpus is never fatal: env-guard.py degrades LOUDLY (stderr) to a built-in environ+.env floor.
